economic_notes: Gwawaenuk Tribe holds investments in Kwa-wa-aineuk Timber GP Ltd. and Kwa-wa-aineuk Timber Limited Partnership, accounted for using the modified equity method. The timber LP had total revenue of $549,371 and a net loss of $126,989 for year ended December 31, 2021. The First Nation also holds investments in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP and related corporation at cost.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 (not 2024). The large prior year revenue ($2,393,763) was driven by a $1,363,411 earnings from investment in Nation business entities (Kwa-wa-aineuk Timber LP), which swung to a loss of $126,889 in 2022 — a significant non-recurring swing but not classified as one-time settlement. Funds held in trust ($66,401) represent Ottawa Trust Fund monies held by Government of Canada. Deferred revenue increased significantly from $19,554 to $252,950 due to unspent ISC and provincial contributions. No long-term debt reported. Accumulated surplus per Note 9 ($2,459,760) differs slightly from the statement figure ($2,441,826) — likely a rounding/presentation difference in the notes. GBEs (Kwa-wa-aineuk Timber LP and GP Ltd.) have a December 31 year-end, creating a three-month lag. No schedule of remuneration for Chief, Council, or staff was included in the document.
